content: Analyse the Q4 2025 of vestas and the possible reasons of big deep into the stocks prices feb 4th 2026.

I'll analyze Vestas' Q4 2025 performance and the reasons behind the stock price decline on February 4, 2026.

Let me start by retrieving Vestas' financial data for Q4 2025 and then look into market data around February 4, 2026.

By

Transcripts Published 02/05/2026, 05:36 AM Earnings call transcript: Vestas Wind Systems Q4 2025 sees stock dip amid mixed results



 

View all comments (0)0

0NMK1.79% Vestas Wind Systems reported its earnings for Q4 2025, revealing a mixed financial performance that led to a 7.22% decline in its stock price. The company’s full-year revenue reached €18.8 billion, a 9% increase year-over-year, while its EBIT margin stood at 5.7%, hitting the upper end of its outlook range. Despite these achievements, the stock’s reaction was negative, reflecting investor concerns over certain aspects of the report.  ...    - Vestas reported a full-year revenue increase of 9% year-over-year. - The EBIT margin reached 5.7%, at the high end of expectations. - Offshore wind turbine deployment is progressing, with profitability targeted by 2027. - The stock price fell by 7.22% following the earnings release. - A record-high order backlog positions Vestas strongly in the wind energy sector.  ...    margin. The company continues to lead the wind energy sector, with a record order backlog of €33.2 billion and a service order backlog of €38.7 billion. However, the Q4 EBIT margin declined by 3.1 percentage points year-over-year,  ...    - Revenue: €18.8 billion for the full year 2025, up 9% year-over-year.  ...    - Gross profit: €2.5 billion, marking an all-time high.
- Return on Capital Employed (ROCE): 11.8%. - Earnings Per Share (EPS): €0.8, a 60% increase from the previous year.  ...    Looking ahead to 2026, Vestas forecasts revenue in the range of €20-22 billion, with an EBIT margin of 6-8%. The  ...    Henrik Andersen, CEO, highlighted the strategic milestones achieved in 2025, stating, "2025 was a year of evidence in  ...    - Declining EBIT margin in Q4 could signal operational challenges.  ...    ## Full transcript - Vestas Wind Systems (0NMK) Q4 2025: Henrik Andersen, CEO, Vestas: Good morning, and welcome to our presentation of our full year 2025, Q4, of course, in  ...    Dreiht. So really appreciated of seeing that. With that, I would like to go to key highlights for the year. So, revenue of the year of EUR 18.8 billion and an EBIT margin of 5.7%. Revenue all-time high from growth in both segments, and  ...    business.  So in Q4 2025, Vestas development generated 102 MW of order intake from Argentina. At the end of Q4, Vestas’  ...    revenue was flat. The EBIT margin of 10% in Q4 is down year-on-year due to higher depreciations and ramp-up cost in  ... results.title: Vestas reports lower Q4 profit but shrugs off Trump's wind critique
highlights:
Vestas reports lower Q4 profit but shrugs off Trump's wind critique | Reuters

Skip to main content Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionalsLearn more aboutRefinitiv Workers use tools to smooth out a blade for a wind-turbine at the Vestas Wind Technology company's factory, located in  ...    - Companies
- Summary - Geopolitical and tariff risks pose uncertainty for wind industry - Company expects improved profitability by 2026 with revenue growth
- Vestas Q4 profit lags forecasts COPENHAGEN, Feb 5 (Reuters) - Wind turbine maker Vestas (VWS.CO), opens new tab reported quarterly profit that fell slightly short of expectations on Thursday, driving its shares down as much as 6% even as the firm's CEO expressed confidence in future growth due to surging electricity demand. Vestas' operating profit before special items in the fourth quarter fell to 580 million euros ($683.59 million) from 759 million a year earlier, against a mean forecast of 597 million euros in an analyst poll provided by Vestas. Sign up here. The softer result was mainly due to higher ramp-up costs of offshore wind projects, rising depreciation stemming from  ...    Henrik Andersen said the unit was expected to be profitable by the end of next year. Vestas' shares were down 4.5% by 1019 GMT, underperforming a 1% drop for the broader Copenhagen benchmark index (.OMXC20CAP), opens new tab with analysts also pointing to a smaller-than-expected 150 million euro share  ...    The global wind industry faces challenges from trade policy concerns and permitting delays, particularly in the United States, where President Donald Trump has sought to shut down offshore projects already under construction and block new ones. "Keep repeating something wrong doesn't make it right," Andersen told Reuters when asked about Trump's characterization  ...    Andersen highlighted Vestas' strong position in the United States, the company's largest market, where deliveries more than doubled in 2025 from a year earlier. He cited surging electricity demand from data centres, AI infrastructure and  ...    On tariffs, Andersen noted that Vestas' manufacturing base in the United States with more than 6,000 employees helped  ...    electricity prices for consumers. The company predicted a full-year 2026 operating profit margin before special items of 6% to 8% and revenue of between 20 billion and 22 billion euros broadly in line with expectations and above the 18.8 billion euros achieved in 2025. ($1 = 0.8485 euros)
